objective: Land the smallest repo-owned Phase 1 artifact slice without introducing speculative runtime or UI semantics.
scope: publish one initial built-in payload schema
check in a sample package with multiple renderer views

workstreams.title: Publish package envelope schema
workstreams.summary: Define a repo-owned manifest schema covering identity, provenance, canonical content, views, and attachments.

workstreams.title: Define one built-in payload contract
workstreams.summary: Capture the minimum payload shape for an implementation plan so later validation and registry work have a stable example.

workstreams.title: Ship a multi-view sample package
workstreams.summary: Provide one package with canonical JSON content plus Markdown, HTML, and JSON projections.

risks.statement: The package fixture could drift away from the accepted contract if later edits treat one renderer as canonical.
risks.mitigation: Keep canonical truth in content/source.json and record every derived view explicitly in manifest.json.

risks.statement: A sample package could accidentally overcommit review-storage or DSL claims that belong to later beads.
risks.mitigation: Keep review state out of the sample and limit this slice to layout, schema, and inspectable views.

exitCriteria: manifest.json points at canonical content and at least three views
the package validates against repo-owned envelope and payload schemas in principle
the sample remains easy to inspect without any runtime-specific tooling
